entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The legume crop being studied for pest resistance and production limits

entities.name: HELICOVERPA ARMIGERA
entities.description: Cotton bollworm, a widely spread pest that limits chickpea production

entities.name: SPODOPTERA EXIGUA
entities.description: Beet armyworm, a serious pest that has emerged in southern India

entities.name: CAMPOLETIS CHLORIDEAE
entities.description: Parasitic wasp, a larval parasitoid that naturally manages pod borer pests

entities.name: SOUTHERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where Spodoptera exigua has emerged as a serious pest

entities.name: INSECTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical agents used for pest control that can lead to the development of resistance

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a pest to withstand the effects of insecticides

entities.name: POD BORER
entities.description: General term for pests that bore into pods, including H. armigera and S. exigua

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Global environmental shifts influencing the need for resilient crop cultivars

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea cultivars to withstand climate change and pest pressure

entities.name: PLANTING DATE
entities.description: The timing of sowing, such as October, December, or January, which affects pest populations

entities.name: ICC 3137
entities.description: A chickpea genotype that recorded the highest number of H. armigera eggs

entities.name: JG 11
entities.description: A chickpea genotype that recorded a low number of H. armigera eggs in 2012

entities.name: ICCV 10
entities.description: A chickpea genotype that recorded a low number of H. armigera eggs in 2013

entities.name: MULTIPLE REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze the interaction between pests and weather patterns

entities.name: WEATHER PATTERNS
entities.description: Environmental factors that have a strong interaction with pest populations
